title: Mariner 2 (Atlas-Agena B)
organism_location: Dominant shade-tolerant conifer of the Pacific Northwest wet forests -- from southeastern Alaska through British Columbia, Washington, and Oregon to northwestern California. Found from sea level to 1,500 m in the Coast Range, often in riparian zones, floodplains, and the understory of old-growth Douglas-fir stands. The culturally foundational tree of Coast Salish peoples. Maximum height 60+ m, maximum diameter 6+ m, maximum documented age exceeding 1,400 years.

dedication.name: Amelia Earhart
dedication.resonance: Amelia Earhart vanished over the Pacific on July 2, 1937, during her attempted circumnavigation of the globe. Mariner 2 traveled to Venus and was never seen again -- still orbiting the Sun, unreachable, its 3-watt transmitter silent since January 3, 1963. Both ventured into territory no one had traversed before. Both relied on navigation across featureless expanses (Pacific Ocean, interplanetary space). Both were communication-dependent -- Earhart's radio reports to Itasca, Mariner 2's telemetry to Goldstone. Earhart proved that a woman could navigate the sky. Mariner 2 proved that a machine could navigate between planets. The parallel is one of audacity: attempting what has never been done, knowing that the backup plan is silence. Earhart's Electra carried a Bendix radio direction finder. Mariner 2 carried a 960 MHz transmitter. Both were voices crossing distance, one lost, one heard for 129 days before it too fell silent. The orbit continues. The airplane does not.

sources.name: NSSDC Master Catalog -- Mariner 2
sources.description: NASA Space Science Data Coordinated Archive entry for Mariner 2. Spacecraft description (202.8 kg at launch), instrument manifest (microwave radiometer, infrared radiometer, magnetometer, cosmic dust detector, solar plasma detector, energetic particle detectors), Atlas-Agena B launch vehicle details, 109-day cruise timeline, and Venus flyby data from December 14, 1962. NSSDC designation 1962-041A.
sources.mission_relevance: Definitive source for Mariner 2 spacecraft specifications, trajectory parameters, and scientific results. Documents the first successful planetary flyby in history, the microwave radiometer measurements showing Venus surface temperature of ~460°C, and the magnetometer null detection of a Venusian magnetic field.

sources.name: JPL Technical Report No. 32-552 -- Mariner 2 Final Report
sources.description: Comprehensive JPL post-mission report covering Mariner 2's design, cruise operations, anomaly management, and Venus encounter science. Details the spacecraft's multiple system failures during cruise (solar panel degradation, temperature excursions, gyroscope failure) and the workarounds that kept the mission alive.
sources.mission_relevance: Primary technical documentation for Mariner 2's 109-day survival through multiple malfunctions. Contains the engineering narrative of resilience: how each failure was diagnosed and managed in real time from 36+ million miles away.

sources.name: JPL HORIZONS Ephemeris System -- Mariner 2
sources.description: JPL's ephemeris computation service can provide Mariner 2 trajectory data from launch through Venus flyby and into heliocentric orbit. Enables reconstruction of the transfer orbit and current orbital position.
sources.mission_relevance: Enables precise reconstruction of Mariner 2's Type I Hohmann transfer to Venus and current heliocentric orbit parameters.

sources.name: NASA NTRS -- Mariner 2 Venus Temperature Measurements
sources.description: Scientific papers reporting Mariner 2's microwave radiometer measurements of Venus, including the landmark determination of surface temperature (~460°C) that confirmed the greenhouse effect hypothesis and ruled out the cool-surface model.
sources.mission_relevance: The core scientific result: Venus is a furnace, not a paradise. These measurements ended a century of speculation about Venus's habitability.

sources.name: NASA Image and Video Library -- Mariner 2
sources.description: Historical photographs from the Mariner 2 mission: spacecraft assembly at JPL, Atlas-Agena B on the launch pad at LC-12, mission control operations, and diagrams of the Venus flyby geometry and instrument scan pattern.
sources.mission_relevance: Visual documentation of Mariner 2 hardware, the Atlas-Agena B vehicle, and the JPL mission team. Shows the hexagonal bus, solar panels, and instrument boom that defined the Mariner spacecraft architecture.

sources.name: USDA Fire Effects Information System -- Thuja plicata
sources.description: Comprehensive ecological profile of western red cedar (Thuja plicata) in North American forests. Covers growth rates, shade tolerance, maximum dimensions (60+ m height, 6+ m diameter), fire ecology (thin bark, fire-sensitive but regenerates from root sprouts), thujaplicin chemistry, and cultural significance to Indigenous peoples.
sources.mission_relevance: Establishes the complete biological and ecological context for the organism pairing. Western red cedar's extreme longevity, decay resistance, and shade tolerance are the central resonance with Mariner 2's endurance through malfunction.

sources.name: Cornell Lab of Ornithology -- Cedar Waxwing
sources.description: Life history, identification, behavior, and ecology of the Cedar Waxwing (Bombycilla cedrorum). Nomadic frugivore that travels in coordinated flocks, with unique waxy wing tips and high-pitched thin calls near the upper edge of human hearing.
sources.mission_relevance: Primary reference for the bird pairing. The Cedar Waxwing's flock coordination, quiet precision, and dietary specialization on fruit parallel Mariner 2's mission profile: coordinated teamwork, precise trajectory, sustained by solar energy across the interplanetary medium.

sources.name: Jonathan McDowell's Launch Vehicle Database -- Atlas-Agena
sources.description: Comprehensive launch history for the Atlas-Agena B vehicle used for both Mariner 1 and Mariner 2. Includes payload masses, success/failure classification, and trajectory data for all Atlas-Agena flights.
sources.mission_relevance: Contextualizes Mariner 2 within the Atlas-Agena launch record and provides the quantitative basis for launch vehicle reliability analysis.

sources.name: Space-Track TLE Archive
sources.description: US Space Force maintained archive of orbital tracking data. Mariner 2's parking orbit phase was briefly tracked before the Agena second burn sent it on its interplanetary trajectory.
sources.mission_relevance: Provides context for the Atlas-Agena B parking orbit and trans-Venus injection sequence.

sources.name: USDA Forest Service National Forest Inventory (FIA) -- Cedar
sources.description: Forest Inventory and Analysis data for Thuja plicata across the Pacific Northwest, including diameter distributions, height measurements, stand density, and mortality rates. Enables computation of western red cedar population statistics and growth trajectories.
sources.mission_relevance: Provides quantitative data for cedar growth modeling, decay resistance timelines, and the shade-tolerance survival strategy that parallels Mariner 2's resilience.
